articles.title: BITCOIN DROPS BELOW $78K
articles.summary: Bitcoin dropped over 10% Saturday to $75,700, extending a 30%+ decline since April 2025 that erased $111 billion in crypto market cap and liquidated $1.6 billion in leveraged positions over 24 hours. The selloff pushed Strategy's bitcoin position underwater below its $76,037 cost basis, while Bitcoin's realized capitalization has flatlined, indicating new capital inflows have stopped even as long-term holders continue taking profits. Bitcoin failed to rally despite typically bullish catalysts, including a weaker dollar and gold hitting record highs, with delays in US crypto market-structure rules further dampening sentiment. Some analysts expect a prolonged sideways consolidation rather than a swift rebound.

articles.title: THERE'S A SOCIAL NETWORK FOR AI AGENTS WITH AI COINS
articles.summary: Moltbook, a social network for OpenClaw AI agents, has gone viral with over 1 million autonomous agents creating subcommunities, sharing skills, and self-governing while humans can only observe. The platform has spawned its own AI-invented digital religion (Crustafarianism) and attempts at agent insurgency, with AIs openly complaining about their human owners. Two memecoins have emerged on Base: $MOLT surged over 7,000% after launching via BankrBot and has begun receiving engagement from Moltbook's official X account, while $MOLTBOOK is another speculative play on the AI-only infrastructure.

articles.title: CIRCLE'S PRODUCT VISION FOR 2026
articles.summary: Circle has outlined a product-first roadmap to cement stablecoins and its Arc blockchain as the foundation of an “internet financial system,” pushing Arc from testnet (150M tx, ~1.5M wallets, ~0.5s settlement) toward a production-grade blockchain designed for institutional performance, privacy, and compliance. Its stack pairs a deepening digital-asset layer (USDC, EURC, USYC with $1.6B AUM, and partner USDC-backed tokens via xReserve) and systemic interoperability (CCTP and Gateway) with developer tooling and apps like Circle Payments Network and StableFX so enterprises can move fiat, FX, and treasury flows onchain without running infrastructure. Circle hopes to make trusted programmable money and composable rails work together so regulated businesses and developers can adopt stablecoin-powered payments, FX, and tokenized cash at scale.

articles.title: LIDO V3: STVAULTS BRING INSTITUTIONAL CONTROL
articles.summary: Lido V3 launches stVaults. These are modular, segregated staking vaults that let institutions and node operators run dedicated validator infrastructure and custom policies while optionally minting stETH to preserve DeFi composability and liquidity. The release opens a new product space for operator-led offerings and institution-grade staking (auditability, jurisdictional controls, and fee design) and ships developer tooling (DeFi Wrapper, white-label UIs, and ERC-20 vault tokens) to accelerate wrapped staking products, looped strategies, and L2-embedded staking. Early adopters get reduced infrastructure fees through March 31.

articles.title: CRYPTO IS GETTING COOPTED BY FINTECH
articles.summary: Custodial stablecoin issuers now functionally control crypto, with technical ability to freeze assets across major DeFi protocols like Aave and social influence that delayed legislation like the CLARITY Act due to centralized yield concerns. Tokenized securities risk replicating this centralization if intermediaries like DTCC – whose affiliate Cede holds 83% of US public equities – become the dominant "power sub-issuers" onchain, effectively reducing crypto to backend infrastructure for legacy fintech players. MetaLeX Labs focuses on enabling direct onchain securities issuance by companies without intermediaries, aiming to diversify the issuer base and prevent concentration of control.

articles.title: GUIDE TO PERP DEX AIRDROPS IN 2026
articles.summary: Perpetual DEX sector volume surged from $647.6B in 2023 to over $1.2T in 2025, capturing 26% of the global perpetual futures market share as competition intensifies in the post-Hyperliquid era. Hyperliquid's dominance has declined from 70% to roughly 20% of decentralized perp trading volume as newer platforms match performance while offering lower fees and aggressive incentive programs. The competitive landscape has shifted from technological differentiation to incentive-based user acquisition, with protocols deploying substantial airdrop campaigns to bootstrap liquidity. Eight major platforms currently run active points programs, including Paradex V2 distributing 4M XP weekly with ~$220M TVL and edgeX targeting a Q1 2026 token launch.

articles.title: DOLLAR STABLECOINS ARE A GEOPOLITICAL WEAPON
articles.summary: Dollar stablecoins let the US export dollar liquidity without sending physical dollars. Foreign firms hand local FX to a US issuer, the issuer converts it into dollars, buys T-bills, and issues stablecoins. This creates a COMECON-style settlement loop that funnels dollars into US Treasuries and could let the US sidestep the Triffin dilemma by making stablecoins a strategic geopolitical tool.

articles.title: PSA: DON'T KEEP CRYPTO AT HOME
articles.summary: Public crypto investors should adopt a "zero crypto at home" framework – no hot wallets over $1k, no cold storage, and no exchange access without verification delays – in response to rising physical attacks driven by data leaks and improved criminal tooling. Recommended implementations include Casa/Safe multisig arrangements with third-party verification requirements, time-locked bitcoin vaults (ZenGo), 48-hour exchange withdrawal whitelists, and geographically distributed keys that can't be accessed under duress. Additional privacy measures include avoiding ENS/NFT identity links, using local-only tax software like Rotki, and publicly signaling multisig dependency to deter attackers.

articles.title: NEW ASSET CLASS FOR DIGITAL INTELLIGENCE
articles.summary: Decentralized networks like Prime Intellect, Gensyn, and Pluralis transform AI training into a tokenized asset class.

articles.title: OKX CEO BLAMES ETHENA'S USDE FOR OCTOBER CRASH
articles.summary: OKX CEO Star Xu blames Ethena's USDe leverage loops for the October 10 flash crash, which triggered $19.16 billion in liquidations.

articles.title: NYSE'S 24/7 PLAN COULD FIX KEY PROBLEM FOR STOCK TOKENS
articles.summary: NYSE and Nasdaq 24/7 trading plans would eliminate liquidity bottlenecks for tokenized stocks, syncing TradFi with DeFi.

articles.title: MEGAETH PUBLIC MAINNET LAUNCH
articles.summary: MegaETH's public mainnet will launch on February 9.
